text: I Like this song and I watch this movie in VHS Tape. Surely you will love this movie if you watch it.....The Never Ending Story....

Remember that in the 1980's, TV's were only 625i (PAL) or 576i (NTSC) in standard, not like the 1080p we have now. And to make sure that all programs (ie movies, ads, etc) kept within the limits of 1980's TV, the "resolution"  was crimped here to 240p or 240i. I'm sure a Blu-Ray version of this movie and video would be in 1080p format. i=interlaced scan (ie draw all odd lines first and 1/50 second later draw all even lines). p=progrsssive scan (ie draw all lines at once every 1/100 seconds).

text: There is a more subliminal meaning to the movie. "Fantasia" refers to our imagination. "The Nothing" represents the emptiness that occurs as a result of a lack of imagination. This idea is based on the notion that people, (especially Americans) have lost interest in literature. The 80's saw the rise of technology- cable TV, video games, electronics, etc. These elements have replaced books over the years. This movie carries a social message- its brilliant!
